query: I'm about to add a `bump` subcommand to our skillrig Go CLI under internal/cli. Before I start, pull up the org's coding rules that apply to this repo so I write it compliant from the get-go.
before i refactor config.ResolveOrigin can you load whatever qodo coding standards we have for /skillrig/cli/
starting work on the TestQuickstart_ integration tests in test/ — what conventions does our team enforce that I should follow up front?
qodo's review keeps flagging FetchSkill for doing an HTTPS clone, but network fetch is literally what the `add` command does. can we fix the rule instead of the code?
that //go:build integration tag rule is wrong for us — we separate integration tests by the test/ directory, no build tag. turn the rule off.
the rule that blocks introducing new go modules is too strict now that we deliberately approved yaml.v3 — downgrade it from error to warning
which qodo rule generated this PR comment about 'verification must be offline and deterministic'? i want to narrow its scope so it stops hitting the fetch layer
our automated reviewer declined PR #8 for three reasons that are all deliberate, documented decisions. help me go through each and decide which governing rules to modify or deactivate.
deactivate qodo rule 782685, it conflicts with the test-layout convention in our CLAUDE.md
this org coding rule is stale — it predates the network boundary we added in 003. edit its content to add a carve-out exception for the clone/sparse-fetch path.
commit this branch, push it, open a PR with the gh cli, and wait for the qodo bot's review to land
take the qodo review findings from PR #8 and post them as inline comments on the github pull request
review my current git diff for correctness bugs and flag anything risky before I push
pull in the terraform-review skill from our skills library and pin it to v1.4.0
run the check that the vendored skills in .agents/skills haven't been tampered with
write a Go function that parses the YAML frontmatter out of a SKILL.md file and returns the metadata.x-skillrig map
explain the GDPR data-retention obligations for our EU customer records
golangci-lint is erroring on an unused variable in internal/cli/root.go, what's the proper way to silence it
rename the field repoPath to originPath everywhere in the internal/config package
how do I point the qodo CLI at a different model in ~/.qodo/config.json?
